methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Medra is a Series A biotech company that has raised $52M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private companies and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, spanning stages from Seed through Series B.

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $104M. Peer-Median analysis references 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies—including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B, Series B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B, Seed), Neko Health ($1.8B, Series B), New Limit ($810M, Series B), and Asimov ($800M, Series B)—producing a median peer valuation of $810M, with a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring using three public Biotech & Health tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) with a P/S median of 6.1x could not be applied due to lack of disclosed target revenue.

The triangulated valuation range is $88M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $457M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation multiples and heterogeneity within the peer set. Key sensitivities include Medra's clinical-stage progress, capital efficiency relative to peer burn rates, and intellectual property scope relative to named competitors.
